Journal.Title: Journal of colloid and interface science

Article.ArticleTitle: Cu-doped NiAl layered double hydroxide enable the synergistic enrichment and hydrogenation of low-concentration nitrate for enhanced photocatalytic ammonia synthesis.

Abstract.AbstractText: Photocatalytic nitrate reduction not only mitigates nitrate pollution in aquatic environments but also offers a promising strategy for ammonia production within the nitrogen cycle. However, the low nitrate concentration in sewage presents a significant challenge for the design of photocatalytic materials. Herein, the NiAl layered double hydroxide (NiAl-LDH) with inherent physical adsorption capacity was employed as photocatalyst, and the incorporation of low-valent Cuδ+ (δ < 2) enhanced the chemisorption for nitrate while simultaneously reduced the hydrogenation reaction barriers of ammonia synthesis under photocatalytic conditions. Therefore, the optimized Cu doped NiAl-LDH achieved an outstanding low-concentration nitrate removal performance (75.6%) with a high selectivity for ammonia synthesis of 90.6%. This study proposes an innovative concept and technical pathway for the photocatalytic treatment and resource recovery of nitrate-containing wastewater in practical scenarios.
